     The Numbers Business Report

Fist Fight (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $32,187,017Details
International Box Office $9,000,000
Worldwide Box Office $41,187,017
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,975,231
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,145,454
Total $7,120,685
Further financial details...

Synopsis

On the last day of the year, mild-mannered high school English teacher Andy Campbell is trying his best to keep it together amidst senior pranks, a dysfunctional administration and budget cuts that put jobs on the line. But things go from bad to worse when he accidentally crosses his much tougher and deeply feared colleague, Ron Strickland, who challenges Campbell to an old-fashioned throw down after school. News of the fight spreads like wildfire and ends up becoming the very thing this school, and Campbell, needed.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,201,873 (37.9% of total gross)
Legs:2.64 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:78.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$25,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,185 opening theaters/3,185 max. theaters, 3.4 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $42,916,020

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: February 17th, 2017 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: February 17th, 2017 (Wide), released as Trenk kaip vyras (Lithuania)
February 17th, 2017 (Wide), released as Между шамарите (Bulgaria)
February 17th, 2017 (Wide), released as UDARI IOT DEDEC (Slovenia)
February 23rd, 2017 (Wide) (Netherlands)
February 24th, 2017 (Wide) (New Zealand)
Video Release: May 16th, 2017 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for language throughout, sexual content/nudity and drug material.
(Rating bulletin 2456 (Cert #50518), 12/21/2016)
Running Time: 91 minutes
Plot point: Bullies, Fired, Revenge, Teachers
Social setting: High School
Subgenre: Farcical / Slapstick Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: New Line Cinema, Village Roadshow Productions, RatPac Entertainment, Dune Entertainment, 21 Laps Entertainment, Wrigley Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

On the last day of the year, mild-mannered high school English teacher Andy Campbell is trying his best to keep it together amidst senior pranks, a dysfunctional administration and budget cuts that put jobs on the line. But things go from bad to worse when he accidentally crosses his much tougher and deeply feared colleague, Ron Strickland, who challenges Campbell to an old-fashioned throw down after school. News of the fight spreads like wildfire and ends up becoming the very thing this school, and Campbell, needed.
